title: Pentaphragma hosei
intro: Pentaphragma hosei is a species of flowering plant in the family Pentaphragmataceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote healing and reduce inflammation, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Pentaphragma hosei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pentaphragma hosei is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as wounds and cuts.
taxonomy: Pentaphragma hosei is classified within the family Cunoniaceae, a family of flowering plants. This family is native to Australia, New Zealand, and parts of the Pacific Islands.
